description: Machine-readable constant-time (CT) evidence freshness + verdict-binding manifest (Bastion B14). Backs docs/CT_VERIFICATION.md / docs/CT_INDEPENDENCE.md and is gated by ci/check_ct_evidence_status.py (also audit_gate.py --ct-evidence-status, principle G-14). Each row binds a CT-sensitive surface to its committed evidence (CT primitive headers + audit regression tests), the verdict tools that establish the claim, a freshness SLO, and a severity. CAAS uses this to know which CT surfaces have current evidence, from which tools, and whether missing/stale/inconclusive evidence blocks or warns.

evaluation_model.committed_evidence: evidence_paths are committed (CT primitive source + audit regression tests) and are checked on every push (cheap): a blocking row fails if a path is missing or last_verified is stale/malformed.
evaluation_model.tool_verdicts: required_tools / optional_tools verdicts (ct-verif, valgrind-ct, dudect, ...) are produced by the heavy CT CI workflows and are NOT committed. The gate evaluates them only when a verdict directory is supplied (--verdict-dir, or the CT workflows). When evaluated: any required-tool FAIL or a single PASS + SKIP is INCONCLUSIVE (never silently PASS); a blocking row fails if its required_tools do not all PASS. When no verdicts are present (push CI), the tool dimension is reported as not_evaluated and only the committed-evidence + freshness dimension gates.
evaluation_model.last_verified: date the committed CT evidence for the surface was last touched/validated (git author date proxy). Tool verdicts carry their own commit/date inside the verdict JSON when evaluated.

severity_legend.blocking: Core secret-bearing CT path; missing/stale committed evidence fails the gate, and a FAIL/inconclusive required-tool verdict fails it when verdicts are evaluated.
severity_legend.warning: CT path with a documented portability/residual caveat; staleness/missing is advisory + pre-alerted.
severity_legend.owner_gated: CT surface whose verdicts are host-only (e.g. --gpu white-box) or otherwise owner-run; reported explicitly and never counted as current evidence on push.

rows.ct_claim: Private key and nonce never branch or index memory data-dependently during ECDSA signing.
rows.notes: CT signing core. Deterministic CT authority is ct-verif + valgrind-ct (blocking CI workflows).

rows.ct_claim: Schnorr signing nonce/secret arithmetic is constant-time; tagged-hash + nonce path branch-free on secrets.

rows.surface: recoverable ECDSA signing (recovery-id derivation must not branch on secrets)
rows.ct_claim: Recoverable signing derives the recovery id without secret-dependent branches.
Public-key derivation from a secret key is constant-time and rejects sk==0 / sk>=n in CT.
rows.notes: Secret-key -> pubkey CT derivation + zero-secret rejection.

rows.ct_claim: Scalar inversion is constant-time on platforms with __int128 (Bitcoin Core targets x86-64 / arm64).
rows.notes: WARNING severity: documented residual RR-NEW-01 (RESIDUAL_RISK_REGISTER.md) — on platforms without __int128 (WASM, MSVC 32-bit) the fallback multiplication chain is variable-time; not a Bitcoin Core target.

rows.surface: nonce derivation / RFC 6979 deterministic path (fixed-iteration CT loop)
rows.ct_claim: RFC 6979 nonce derivation runs a fixed-iteration constant-time loop; no secret-dependent retry timing.

rows.surface: CT vs variable-time boundary on GPU (verify = public data; GPU CT-uniformity is host-only)
rows.ct_claim: GPU verify kernels operate on public data (variable-time is correct); secret-bearing GPU paths are warp-uniform / branchless, validated by host-only --gpu gates.
rows.arch_compiler: GPU (CUDA/OpenCL/Metal); white-box ncu requires a --gpu host
rows.notes: Verify paths are public-data (variable-time correct by design). The white-box GPU CT-uniformity gate (check_gpu_ct_uniformity.py) is host-only (--gpu) and owner-run; the static branch lint (check_ct_branches.py) runs in CI. Never counted as current verdict evidence on push.
